records.ety: [L. subsidiarius: cf. F. subsidiaire. See Subsidy.]

defs: Furnishing aid; assisting; auxiliary; helping; tributary; especially, aiding in an inferior position or capacity; as, a subsidiary stream.
quotes: Chief ruler and principal head everywhere, not suffragant and subsidiary.
They constituted a useful subsidiary testimony of another state of existence.

defs: Of or pertaining to a subsidy; constituting a subsidy; being a part of, or of the nature of, a subsidy; as, subsidiary payments to an ally.
quotes: George the Second relied on his subsidiary treaties.

defs: One who, or that which, contributes aid or additional supplies; an assistant; an auxiliary.
